description:
Remove `'unsafe-inline'` from the production CSP and switch from Report-Only to enforcing. The earlier fix (commit ca04a5e on the reverted security-fixes branch) dropped `'unsafe-eval'` but left both `script-src 'unsafe-inline'` and `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` because the inline-handler/style audit was never done.

## Scope (inventoried 2026-05-15)

Across `app/templates/`:
- 514 inline event handlers (`onclick=`, `onchange=`, `onsubmit=`, etc) — these CANNOT be nonced. Only fix is refactor each to `addEventListener` in an external JS file. The biggest line item.
- 153 templates with `style="..."` attributes — same problem for style-src. Only fix is move to `b-*` utility classes (or accept `'unsafe-inline'` in style-src indefinitely).
- 57 templates with `<style>` blocks — these CAN be nonced, but the cost is a per-request nonce middleware in the app + Jinja context wiring.
- A small handful of `<script>` blocks (mostly in `base.html`) — same: noncable.

## Multi-step plan (each step its own session)

**Step 1 — CSP nonce middleware (no breaking changes yet):**
- Add `app/middleware/csp_nonce.py` that generates a per-request nonce, stores it in `request.state.csp_nonce`, and adds an `add_header Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only ... 'nonce-XXX' 'unsafe-inline' ...` (both nonce and unsafe-inline so legacy inline still works during rollout).
- Wire the nonce into Jinja context via a `template_context` dependency.
- Update all `<script>` and `<style>` blocks in templates to include `nonce="{{ csp_nonce }}"`.
- Note: with nonce + unsafe-inline both present, modern browsers honor the nonce and ignore unsafe-inline. Legacy browsers still get unsafe-inline. So this is non-breaking but starts enforcing nonces silently.
- Verify in browser devtools that no CSP report-only violations show up for noncable inline content.

**Step 2 — Refactor inline event handlers to `addEventListener` (the big refactor):**
- 514 sites across `app/templates/`. Convert each `onclick="foo()"` to a `data-action="foo"` attribute, with a single delegated listener at the bottom of `base.html` that dispatches by attribute.
- Best done with parallel agents working on file batches (e.g. one agent per top-level dir under `templates/`).
- Test each affected page after conversion.

**Step 3 — Refactor inline `style=` to `b-*` utility classes:**
- 153 templates. Most uses are layout (display:flex, gap:..., width:..., etc) that map cleanly to existing `b-*` classes or new utility classes.
- Audit, define new utility classes for repeated patterns, replace.

**Step 4 — Drop `'unsafe-inline'`, switch CSP from Report-Only to enforcing:**
- Once steps 1-3 are done, CSP becomes `default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'nonce-XXX'; style-src 'self' 'nonce-XXX' https://fonts.googleapis.com; ...`.
- Switch the header name from `Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only` to `Content-Security-Policy` in `/opt/edge/nginx/conf.d/vigilant.conf`.
- Verify no CSP violations in browser console for any template.

## Why split

Doing all four steps in one shot risks shipping a CSP that breaks 1 of 514 inline handlers silently and the operator finds out from a user report 3 days later. Each step is independently shippable and reversible. Step 1 alone is genuinely useful (per-request nonces enable Steps 2-4 incrementally). Step 2 is the bulk and benefits from parallel agents.

## Out of scope for this ticket
- Tightening `connect-src` / `img-src` / `font-src` further than current Report-Only policy
- Removing `https://fonts.googleapis.com` / `https://fonts.gstatic.com` allowances (would require self-hosting JetBrains Mono — separate UX/perf decision)
title: Remove CSP unsafe-inline (VVP-2026-007)
